commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=552ad0e710cafb78550da747f2bdcd52133730f6)
commands.review_summary:
This branch changes OpenRouter API model-id normalization to preserve single-segment `openrouter/...` IDs and adds unit tests for prefixed, namespaced, special, and malformed OpenRouter IDs.

PR surface: Source +5, Tests +65. Total +70 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and PR head preserve `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` as the API-facing id, while the public OpenRouter model list exposes `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`. I did not run an authenticated live OpenRouter completion in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Human review is needed because the branch preserves the broken API id, lacks real provider-path proof, and competes with other open candidate fixes for the same linked issue.

target.title: fix(openrouter): prevent model prefix duplication for short canonical IDs
target.body:
## Summary

- **Problem:** When using short canonical model IDs like `openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash`, the gateway incorrectly doubles the provider prefix in the API call, resulting in `openrouter/openrouter/deepseek-v4-flash` being sent to OpenRouter.
- **Fix:** Modified `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` to correctly handle short canonical model IDs by keeping the `openrouter/` prefix for non-namespaced models.
- **Out of scope:** No changes to namespaced model IDs (e.g., `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`) or special models (e.g., `auto`, `fusion`).

## Linked context

Fixes #95198

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** OpenRouter model prefix duplication when using short canonical model IDs.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux (NewStartOS V4.4.2-ZTE), Node.js v24.13.1, OpenClaw latest main (commit 552ad0e710).
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts` with 12 test cases covering short canonical IDs, namespaced IDs, and special models.
- **Evidence after fix:**
```
$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts

 RUN  v4.1.8 /home/0668001110/ZTEProject/openclaw-worktrees/issue-95198

 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  12 passed (12)
   Start at  14:49:46
   Duration  949ms
```
- **Observed result after fix:** The `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` function correctly handles short canonical model IDs by keeping the `openrouter/` prefix, preventing duplication when the gateway adds the provider prefix.
- **What was not tested:** Live OpenRouter API calls (requires API key). The test validates the normalization logic without requiring a live OpenRouter connection.

## Regression Test Plan

- Coverage level: Unit test
- Target test file: `extensions/openrouter/models.test.ts`
- Scenario locked in: Short canonical model ID normalization
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Tests verify the exact normalization logic for different model ID formats.

## Root Cause

- Root cause: `normalizeOpenRouterApiModelId` was stripping `openrouter/` prefix from short canonical model IDs, causing the gateway to add it again.
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test coverage for short canonical model ID normalization.

## Current review state

- [x] AI-assisted (built with Claude Code)
